text:
Hunter × Hunter: Real Stage; A Longing for Pakunoda ~ A Spider's Memory, (リアルステージ ハンター × ハンター「A Longing for Phalcnothdk, 〜蜘蛛の記憶〜」) is a stage play featuring the Hunter × Hunter series. The play is a retelling of the events of when Kurapika captures Chrollo and forces the Phantom Troupe to do a hostage exchange, in a more serious and darker tone.

Information

The stage play is an alternate retelling of the end of the Yorknew City arc, where Kurapika captures Chrollo and forces the Phantom Troupe to exchange their hostages of Gon and Killua for their leader. This play also features flashbacks of before the Phantom Troupe first came to be.

The Stage Play was originally performed from August 13 th -29 th of 2004.

Summary

The play can be separated into four parts revolving around Pakunoda's memories. Alternatively, it is divided into 11 parts based on the storyline.

Opening: Operation carried out

After Nobunaga defeats an enemy, Pakunoda uses her Nen ability to probe his memories about Kurapika. She then employs her Memory Bomb to reveal Kurapika’s face and name to Machi, Nobunaga, Shizuku, and Chrollo. The stage abruptly darkens. The play shifts to the moment of Chrollo’s capture by Kurapika, juxtaposed with Gon and Killua’s attempted escape — represented through a slow, stylized dance under dim lighting. With their leader in chains, Machi, Nobunaga, Shizuku, Chrollo, joined by Shalnark and Phinks, reflect grimly on the dire situation confronting them.

Gon and Killua are held captive and are guarded by Shizuku, Machi, Shalnark, Pakunoda, and Phinks. They are accompanied by a non-canon character called "The contact man" who keeps shouting until Phinks silences him with a punch. The man’s phone rings repeatedly, further annoying Phinks, who orders him to pick it up. When he does, the call turns out to be for Pakunoda. Though the phone is passed to her, Phinks ultimately snatches it away. The rest of the scene centers on the phone call between Phinks and Kurapika as well as the phone call between Pakunoda and Kurapika, portrayed with a surprising comedic twist despite the tense circumstances. Amidst the rain, Pakunoda remembers back the old days.

Pakunoda's Memory 1

On a rainy day in Meteor City, the Phantom Troupe rummaged through the garbage. Amid the refuse, Shizuku discovered a dead baby, bringing a fleeting moment of sadness to the group. Shortly after, Machi spotted a spider crawling nearby, which sparked a brief thrill among them. Chrollo arrived and ordered everyone to stop fooling around, while Shizuku continued to toy with the baby until Machi quickly intervened and put it aside. Calmly, Chrollo picked up the baby, calling it a brother to all of them, and began a speech about the harsh land they were born into. As he finished, he tore away the chains binding the stage—and in that instant, the lights went out.

Chrollo vs. Kurapika

The scene opens with Chrollo, bound in chains, casually mocking the situation Kurapika has imposed, joking that “it’s like a coffee break.” Enraged, Kurapika strikes him several times, but restrains himself from delivering a fatal blow, choosing instead to spare Chrollo in order to protect his friends.

Nobunaga vs. Phinks

The Troupe members argued over what to do with Pakunoda, and Shizuku intervened, suggesting a coin toss to settle the dispute. But when she threw the coin, it never landed, sparking a chaotic brawl that centered on Phinks versus Nobunaga. Amid the mayhem, the contact man, was comically jostled by everyone. Phinks punched him, Shizuku delivered a playful blow, Shalnark slapped him, and Machi kneed him.

Despite the relentless bullying, he attempted to defend himself, claiming he hadn’t done anything wrong, while the troupe surrounded him with weapons. In a slow, choreographed sequence, he dodged their attacks, only to be beaten again—his face battered, body pierced, and heart crushed by Phinks, before Machi suspended him with her threads.

At that moment, Gon and Killua appeared, still tied up. When the phone rang and no one wanted to answer it, the Contact Man reluctantly picked it up and, at Kurapika’s request, handed it first to Killua and then to Gon.

Pakunoda's Memory 2

Shalnark, acting playfully with binoculars, annoys Pakunoda and Chrollo while trying in vain to reach Phinks over the radio. Suddenly, Machi bursts in, warning everyone to run as a looming threat closes in. With the sound of danger drawing nearer, Shizuku and Nobunaga arrive just as a gravely injured Phinks staggers inside. A voice outside declares the building surrounded, urging them to surrender. Phinks, blaming himself for his failed mission, volunteers for a suicidal last stand. Pakunoda protests, but Chrollo insists the survival of the Spider comes before all else. Phinks’ sacrifice gives the troupe the chance to escape, yet as they mourn him back at their hideout, he reappears—battered and near death. Chrollo embraces him, and as Phinks’ torn clothes slip away, the spider tattoo on his back is revealed.

8 o'clock at Lingon Airport

The scene opens on Pakunoda standing with Kurapika, Chrollo bound tightly in chains beside them. Kurapika lays down his rules with cold precision, making it clear that Pakunoda must follow them without exception. For a brief moment, doubt flickers in his eyes—he reminds himself that above all, his mission is to save his friends. With no choice but to comply, Pakunoda accepts the terms and makes her way back to the troupe’s hideout.

Gon vs. Phantom Troupe

At the hideout, tension rises as Phinks grows furious over Chrollo’s absence, sparking a heated dispute among the troupe. Gon suddenly steps in, demanding they show more understanding toward Pakunoda, but he quickly backs down when Phinks threatens him. The standoff is broken by the reappearance of the contact man, his phone ringing. At Kurapika’s request, he hands it to Gon, who confirms that all the members are gathered. Gon then passes the phone to Phinks, who grudgingly agrees to release Pakunoda.

As they prepare to leave, Gon unexpectedly strikes Phinks before retreating with his friends. The contact man tries to slip away with them, but Gon halts him with a sharp question: “What if Kurapika calls again?”—forcing him to stay behind. When Pakunoda takes a wrong turn, the group is forced to double back, leading to another clash where Gon once again hits Phinks. And once more, when the contact man attempts to sneak off with them, Gon firmly stops him.

Pakunoda's Memory 3

The troupe gathers in silence, mourning the loss of Uvogin. Nobunaga pours alcohol onto his grave before giving a brief, heartfelt tribute. Phinks follows with his own words, grief sharp in his tone. Driven by rage, Nobunaga vows to hunt down the chain user, cutting down anyone who stands in his way. His declaration sparks a heated dispute within the group—whether to pursue Uvogin’s killer or not. Tension crackles, especially between Chrollo and Nobunaga, their opposing wills nearly boiling over. The scene closes with Chrollo stepping forward quietly, placing a single flower at the grave in solemn respect.

Hostage exchange

Aboard the airship, Gon, Killua, Pakunoda, Chrollo, and Kurapika gather for the hostage exchange. The trade is made in tense silence, broken only when Gon presses Chrollo to say something—reminding him that Pakunoda had saved his life. Kurapika then confronts Chrollo, speaking of the misfortune and suffering his actions brought upon him. Wordless, Chrollo retrieves his bag, then calmly opens the airship hatch. Just before leaping, he turns back one last time—coldly revealing that it was he who had ordered the slaughter of the Kurta clan.

Memory bullet (memory bomb)

The scene shifts back to the hideout, where the troupe seethes with anger over Chrollo’s absence. Suspicion turns toward Pakunoda, and they warn her to be ready to die depending on her answer. Calm but resolute, she raises her gun—not at an enemy, but at her own comrades—and urges them to trust her. With steady conviction, she declares that she entrusts them with her feelings, then pulls the trigger. One by one, memories flood into the troupe as Pakunoda collapses. Her life ends in that instant, sacrificed so her comrades may carry her memories within them.

Epilogue (Pakunoda's Memory 4)

The finale opens with Gon and Killua finally free, resting in relief. Their peace is broken when the contact man suddenly appears, his face covered in patches. He approaches them awkwardly, mimicking Phinks’ gruff gratitude as he relays a message of thanks. Each time he tries to slip away, the boys call him back, sending him running back and forth in exasperation. When asked about Pakunoda, his expression hardens—he admits she is dead before fleeing.

The scene then shifts to a memory of Pakunoda escorting Gon and Killua. She wonders aloud why they don’t simply escape, knowing full well they could. They answer quietly: as Kurapika’s friends, they don’t want to see him forced to kill. Elsewhere, the contact man finally finds himself alone. His phone rings, and fear grips him—until he realizes that the caller was his buddy. With trembling relief, he answers. Soon, Gon and Killua enter. The entire cast follow as they gather once more on stage, striking their final poses together.

Cast

Gon Freecss – Junko Takeuchi
Killua Zoldyck – Kanako Mitsuhashi
Kurapika – Akiko Kimura
Chrollo Lucilfer – Ryosei Konishi
Pakunoda – Ikeda Yukiko
Nobunaga Hazama – Kagawa Kouji
Machi Komacine – Kuon Sayaka
Shizuku Murasaki – Nasu Megumi
Phinks Magcub – Satou Takuyuki
Shalnark – Ichikawa Kuniyoshi
The man-caught-in-between/The contact man – Kamioka Takahiro

Trivia

This is the 3rd stage play in the Hunter × Hunter stage adaptations.
Only Gon and Killua's 1999 adaptation anime voice actresses reprise their respective roles.
Kurapika is played by Mito's and Biscuit's from Greed Island Final OVA 1999 anime adaptation voice actress Akiko Kimura.
Feitan, Franklin, Bonolenov, Kortopi, Leorio, Hisoka, Illumi, Kalluto, and Melody although having roles in the original story do not appear in this play.
Unlike the other plays, there are no musical numbers.
Franklin's speech where he asks Shalnark what the worst possible situation would happen to them all was done by Shizuku in the play.
An original character simply called, The man-caught-in-between/The contact man was created just for the play.
The play was performed at the Sun-mall Theater in Shinjuku, Tokyo.
This is the last Hunter × Hunter related play to be performed until, Hunter × Hunter: The Stage.
